payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So, without further ado, let's go and kick into the recap. So, the party has initially been sent down on a mission by the arcanist Allura Vysoren in search of a halfling paladin who has gone missing, named Lady Kima of Vord. A folk hero and a very respected individual, she went on a vision quest and apparently went alone into Kraghammer seeking some sort of great evil that was festering beneath the city, and hasn't been seen for weeks and weeks. The party was hired by her to go and try to find her whereabouts and hopefully bring her back safely. Since traversing and meeting individuals in Kraghammer, they then spearheaded into the Greyspine Mines where apparently these monstrosities have been burbling out time and time again over the past few weeks. Pushing down, they had encountered umber hulks, they managed to inquire some duergar, which, apparently there's a lot of them down here. Duergar being an ashy-skinned, subterranean dark, evil dwarf-type offshoot. One of which had with it a brain-type quadruped that managed to Brain Spike Grog and send him catatonic for a short time. They, wandering through the caverns, discovered there was a duergar war camp across the way from-- across this large chasm that was currently being run by a general duergar alongside what appeared to be, and what they've now discovered to be, a mind flayer or an illithid. They managed to, upon looking deeper into this cavern, finding hidden behind a waterfall, thanks to the druid and the ranger, completely stealing from the rest of the group their flying carpet, made a very tenuous relationship and alignment with another mind flayer, apparently cast out of his own society, searching to find a way to both get revenge and find his way back into his colony of people. And his name was Clarota, lovingly called Clarence by the group, probably much to his own chagrin. After which they decided to go ahead and try to extract information from this duergar general, and stop this war camp from preparing itself for battle and attack, seemingly, on Kraghammer in the near future. Through a strange, crazy plan they came up with, they managed to fly undetected over via flying carpet and eagle, and floating sorcerer, over the entire war camp, made a Metal Gear run into the center of their main barracks-- (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, guys. So we finally have Pike's video finished, so I hope you got to enjoy that. As a heads-up, too, unfortunately we're going to be down one Scanlan due to work, but that's okay, the bard will be in our hearts this evening, and you're welcome to tweet at him angrily to make sure he makes the next session.

payload.raw_text: -- after which, Vax bisected it, pretty much, in one surprise round of attacks that led to a large battle in the center of this building, partway through which ground began to rumble. And then digging a forth from underneath, a bulette, a gargantuan armored, burrowing creature that was hungry and very dangerous that then made the battle that much more intense. A heavy, blood-filled battle later, involved with Percy getting his hand caught and firing his gun into the gullet of the beast, eventually it had its armor pulled apart and was defeated by the party. The remnants of the duergar army inside of this building have been destroyed, aside from a few stragglers who have laid their weapons down, and the general now lies beaten, on his knees before the rest of the party. And that is where we pick up and begin. So, as you all are catching your breath, breathing heavily, as the adrenaline slowly leaves the system. Looking around this building, with the large hole still glaring through the roof.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: -- threw a bucket of goblin shit into the face of the one mind flayer--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns_last5.text: Whoops.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "And I'll give you the secret entrance to Emberhold itself, leading you straight within the throne room."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, through the lake. Deep below, you'll find a tunnel. This tunnel will lead you to a subterranean passage straight to the stronghold."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we check to make sure he's not untruthful?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, he's bullshitting through his teeth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hey, he seemed honest about the passage but dishonest about the-- he's basically following her lead. "Uh-huh."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell us true, where is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If I tell you, will you let me live?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can lock him up. And then kill him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Don't just leave me alive. Give me a chance to walk from this building, on my own, arms intact."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, you start-- It's really tough hide. You know, 'cause you tried to cut through before and you managed to make a few marks. It takes you a few minutes. You manage to get, let's say, about three saucer-shaped pieces of armor out of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: On the left side of the keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Can I ask a question?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there a lot of other soldiers still in the camp, or did we--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, there are plenty of soldiers still in the camp.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit! We should've kept him alive, you guys! Okay, okay, okay. Yes, Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, he was about to walk out the door and cause mayhem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true. He was gonna alert everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I harvest any natural armor from the bulette? The kitty?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can certainly try. There's plates that have been broken and cracked. You can try and start cleaving away at it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right, yes, I start butchering, or something, the bulette.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: However, as the javelins strike all you guys, moments later there's a gargantuan explosion, and there's a flash of reddish light from below. A giant fiery blooming explosion bursts into the center of that area. You can see duergar thrown left and right. You manage to take out about 12 of them in that one blast.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we healed the other game. Even with that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, the three that are going towards Grog--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That-- Natural 20. A 17, what's your AC?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That one misses, and the last one-- Yeah, two natural 20s.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Good thing you got a thousand hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's the point of a meat tank.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take a total of 36 points of piercing damage, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And he still has more hitpoints than we do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If Scanlan were here, he'd moon and blind all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He is here, Scanlan's holding on tight as--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: -- it's now able to be healed completely, so. Clarota takes a moment and kind of grasps the wound.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Breaking it off is not gonna work.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have to pull it from the wound.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. You take your moment, you view up where the puncture wound is, with a little bit of Grog's help, you both grab the edge, and you're like, 'On three. One, two, three, pull!' You pull through the wound, not against the arrow--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- 'cause that would just tear everything out in the process, but nevertheless, in doing so, Clarota does take an additional eight points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But, the wound is now no longer a giant--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: For the most part, we've got a few rolls, and it worked fine. Looking inside though, this camp looks like it's been abandoned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Clarota gets up on the carpet and-- "Thank you." The carpet kind of drifts alongside you guys. You guys are pushing backwards towards the cavern. The tunnel kind of begins to pinch a little bit. It gets to about a 15-foot width before it opens up again. You're about a hundred feet back. You can now see it opening into a-- what looks like an extension of the war camp. (all groan)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should've gone down! What were we thinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he said go down. We didn't go down.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 13 for me. I like how my passive perception is way higher than any rolls that I ever do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I do keep that-- I keep tabs on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we all doing perception rolls?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It looks like it was built, and then the expansion into the war camp you just escaped from seemed to be the next step. Like it's slowly advancing and this section of it has been in disuse. As you push in towards the center of the camp you can see there's older campfires, lots of the tents have like partially collapsed. Dust has gathered.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should've gone down! What were we thinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he said go down. We didn't go down.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 13 for me. I like how my passive perception is way higher than any rolls that I ever do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I do keep that-- I keep tabs on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we all doing perception rolls?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For the most part, we've got a few rolls, and it worked fine. Looking inside though, this camp looks like it's been abandoned.

payload.raw_text: As the evening transpires, you all get your evenings of rest, you guys kind of keeping watch, gambling away. At a certain point, throughout the evening, something perks up your ears. You begin hearing footsteps, many footsteps.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Is that cool?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: The next one will be better.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right. Did we all rest? Are we all rested?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yep.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As the footsteps approach the outskirts of the alcove, the rest of you sleeping soundly, unaware of what's transpiring-- A few moments pass, and the footprints appear to be passing, past, downward into the tunnel. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Did eyebrows go up?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (whistles In the Hall of the Mountain King)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The footsteps progress through, and you-- there's no light source, as duergar for the most part do not require light to see in the tunnels.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we see through the dark-- but we can see in the dark?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys have low-light vision, not dark vision.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's no light in this area at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it is pitch black. You just hear--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- you just hear the footprints go by.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good thing we didn't make a campfire!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear them approach the outskirts of the alcove.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have dark vision, gosh dang it!

payload.raw_text: All right. So. Eventually, when you feel enough time has passed, where those who've been resting come from their sleep, spells recovered, wounds are healed, except for those who didn't sleep, you guys maintain the same health you had previously.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: As the footsteps approach the outskirts of the alcove, the rest of you sleeping soundly, unaware of what's transpiring-- A few moments pass, and the footprints appear to be passing, past, downward into the tunnel. (all laugh)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Eventually, when the footfalls dissipate, and silence once again takes the camp, you breathe a sigh of relief and remove your hand from Vex's face.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Well, maybe just leave it there.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Last time I did that I got kicked in the nuts, very hard.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: And liked it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. So. Eventually, when you feel enough time has passed, where those who've been resting come from their sleep, spells recovered, wounds are healed, except for those who didn't sleep, you guys maintain the same health you had previously.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it is pitch black. You just hear--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- you just hear the footprints go by.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good thing we didn't make a campfire!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear them approach the outskirts of the alcove.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have dark vision, gosh dang it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the footsteps approach the outskirts of the alcove, the rest of you sleeping soundly, unaware of what's transpiring-- A few moments pass, and the footprints appear to be passing, past, downward into the tunnel. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eventually, when the footfalls dissipate, and silence once again takes the camp, you breathe a sigh of relief and remove your hand from Vex's face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, maybe just leave it there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Last time I did that I got kicked in the nuts, very hard.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And so we will pick it back up. So as you all regain consciousness and prepare yourselves for the day ahead, once again, you've gone a number of days without sunlight, without any vision of the sky. It's a very disorienting experience for all of you who are unfamiliar with subterranean territory. Even though you are rested, you don't feel rested. There's kinks in your back from the rocky floor. The best you could find yourself any sort of comfort here is from whatever you have on your back. Most of you sleep in your armor out of necessity and paranoia. As consciousness comes to you, you guys are able to try and find your way back out now that you've been sealed in.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Great. If the mods want to post the command 'criticalrole', you guys can link to that charity and we'll go ahead and roll those videos so you guys can get updated on the characters, and we'll be back in a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: See you guys in a little bit. [character intros]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, guys. All right, I think everyone is sufficiently rested and bathroom breaked.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys, you take five or six minutes or so. You manage to hack away enough in one of the pillars to leave enough room to squeeze through. It takes a while. You manage to cleave that open. You all gather your things, you group together and you can continue in whichever direction you wish.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I just give it a try, just to see? Try make a dent.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, go ahead and make an athletics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And just to try it out, I would like bring out the flaming hammer of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Actually, for this I would say it's a straight strength check. This is just physicality, strength against the stonework.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Might need to take your armor off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so as Pike-- as you go and swing at the pillar, you impact with all your might and the pillar cracks at the impact. You're like, 'that is awesome', then you look up a few inches and notice that Grog's ax is also in the same pillar.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yay, you loosened it up for me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, you guys step back into the tunnel, all of you squeezing through with the rest of your materials, heading further down. About 15 minutes of travel go by and the very air itself is becoming steadily warmer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- of the scenery around you, you take a moment. What's the reach of that one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just read it, where is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Three miles. The natural entities that you can sense in this cavern system are the few clusters of bats that exist in the back portion of the war camp that you guys just ran from. That's about it, I mean, there are a few other strange things that you've seen that live down here, but they wouldn't be anything that you would naturally sense.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Lots of bats. They're very nice bats

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Very pleasant, they're very friendly. That's about it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for your positive energy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys come rushing in, let's go ahead and roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cart. Could be some sort of device--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From around the edge, you can just barely make out these two lumbering humanoid shapes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we tell what they are?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he's trying to see if they're seeing us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They begin to step towards your direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys back and kind of flatten yourself against the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can see they're now both dragging behind them this small roughshod cart, with something inside it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We string a rope across the tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I also yank my serpentine belt off--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And throw it out, right under the rope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, as he throws the leather, the leather shifts form suddenly in midair into a serpent that slips onto the ground, and slithers in place, curling up at the approaching two monolithic entities.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is gonna go really poorly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As they begin to cross in front of you, you both pull taut on the rope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The immediate one falls forward, tripping face-first on the ground, a heavy (impact sound). You guys hear in the distance this loud thud, impact sound.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we call, I go, Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I leap out, daggers at the fallen one.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You do, actually! I'm rolling initiative, so we have the order. You guys are gonna have a full round of surprise on them first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is gonna go really poorly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As they begin to cross in front of you, you both pull taut on the rope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The immediate one falls forward, tripping face-first on the ground, a heavy (impact sound). You guys hear in the distance this loud thud, impact sound.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we call, I go, Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I leap out, daggers at the fallen one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys come rushing in, let's go ahead and roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, you know, when the ladies call my name.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's not too bad, could be worse.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What are we fighting? What are we fighting?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, right? Oh god, here we go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What are we fighting, we don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you better be throwing bitches into that lava.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Last time I tried to do somebody to something, I did it to myself, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys, coming in this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bitches in lava, bitches in lava.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I got 99 problems and a troll ain't one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are they trolls, are they ogres?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god, one's on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's huge, these are really big.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One is back here and one's just fallen over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And they are carrying some sort of large cart-like object, I'll just say for now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is so dumb, why did we do this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let's say it's about there. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do we get the surprise at all?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Still standing. Barely. It's now its turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: -- plus five, ten! I rarely do this one, kids.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: 17 on the second one. And 17 on the second one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, is there a saving throw for half damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No! I mean, 12 on the third one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Is there a saving throw on this one, or is it just straight damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you, you blast the ogre with three. As each explosion hits, you see the rippling of its kind of grotesque flesh from the impact, the third one blasting off a portion of its shoulder, exposing this meaty bone and blood. It gives out this horrible (roars) into the tunnel itself, causing just the atmosphere to shake, as bits of stone begin to rumble and fall from the ceiling. All right, that ends your turn?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Both of its claws are at the edges of the lava, and it's pulling itself, screeching, its mouth and eyes wild with the severe pain it's feeling as it's slowly melting him from the bottom. The arrow (impact sound) into its shoulder and burst into flame. It's now freaking out. It looks like it's having a really, really rough time making its way out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits. So the first one, you fire towards it, except the arrow just (whoosh) into the lava and (puff).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With a slight puff of smoke.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I have used my flaming bowstring on the second one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On the second one? If you'd like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sweet. Oh! And I Hunter's Marked him, because I can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you moved the Hunter's Mark over, he is now your quarry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll the additional fire damage and your quarry damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 22 damage, all righty. The one arrow disappears. The second one, as you release it from the bowstring, you feel the slight tingle of the arcane energy released into the arrow. The arrow bursts into flame as it releases from your bow, streaking and piercing the front of its hide as it's trying to claw its way out.

payload.raw_text: As Grog, you come to consciousness again--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Trinket's on the sidelines, man.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: That makes me so sad!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: We're not going back.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: And it's all you guys' fault!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: But it was like three years, Trinket was by your side. This is the first time ever.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As Grog, you come to consciousness again--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go over to Grog and Cure Wounds at fourth level.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You tried to kill us, but you failed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I did a pretty decent job, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I ate pizza in front of you guys. I don't care. It was tasty. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. We're not gonna have Trinket this whole thing. We better get back to Trinket soon. I'm freaking out, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true, if we keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I might be able to guide him--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And it's all you guys' fault!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But it was like three years, Trinket was by your side. This is the first time ever.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. As you guys take a moment to heal yourselves, you pull yourselves back up to your feet, and you tread further down this tunnel. Another 25-30 minutes of the slight curvature, and it gets steeper and steeper, until eventually it opens up into a familiar cavern to some of you. You see a large waterfall pouring down from above--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you'd like to, yeah. So mark that in your inventory, you have some hand-sized shards of broken glass.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And two to Pike for healing me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Anything else you guys wish to do in this section?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So we searched the ogres, that's it. I guess we should just start walking further down.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You take a poo on the black puddle of acid?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think-- Well, no. That's the only heat source, that's the only lava there was, and now it's cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And now it has cooled, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's all right, I think it's worth it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to take a hit dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and do that. As many as you'd like.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's rest for a half-hour or so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Take a moment, you can spend any hit dice now that you need to to heal yourself. Just make sure to mark how many you've used.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I just hear the sounds of ones.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the base
payload.raw_text: As you make your way to the base of the watery lake, at the bottom, you can see a swish to the water, and a very familiar sight briefly shows itself in the distance. As you see a slick domed fish-like head protrude with a few tentacles in the distance, you all instinctively step quite a distance away, and it seems to withdraw back into the water.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right, so you, taking the flying carpet, it's a very precarious journey as the weight limit for the carpet--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: You go on it, right?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: He's with me, and he cast Featherfall.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You can go ahead and cast Fly on your bear. Trinket awkwardly (bear noises) manages to swim her way down to the bottom floor.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Come, Trinket, come.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So the chat room creature choice, those will be an occasional thing, not a weekly thing, 'cause it's too difficult to keep up with that and a little too random. Plus that's just too many creatures to be prepared for. But every few weeks or so, I'm gonna throw one out there for you guys. So come and watch. Hopefully you'll be there for one of those episodes. Put your friends together, vote for the creature you want to see them fight, and if a TPK happens, not my fault, it's totally on you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we've got a little bit of a ways to go, but I'm going to go ahead and have these guys sign this one as well, and I'm going to push you guys that by the end of tonight, if we reach 1,550, I'll go ahead and throw this in with the next promo kit, as well. And if not I'll hang onto it for myself, and I won.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Thank you guys so much. That was fun. It gets more intense like every week.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's apparently the path this is taking, so--

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: And just so you guys know, that was the chat room that decided on your fate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, second week in a row.
